old_claim.note: Nanotechnology (Early Research) is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because advanced materials science is a broad parent field and should not depend on one later subfield label.

source_shape.source_locator: Nanotechnology history review establishing nanotechnology as a specific later field within broader materials and physical sciences.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The review treats nanotechnology as a later specialized field, not as the prerequisite root of all advanced materials science.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The graph should avoid broad-field depends-on-narrow-subfield edges; nanotechnology can be an included branch or downstream specialization of advanced materials science.

ontology_before: Advanced materials science depended on nanotechnology, reversing parent/child field semantics and creating temporal tension with earlier materials nodes.
ontology_after: Advanced materials science now depends on advanced chemistry, quantum physics, and synthetic materials while nanotechnology remains a connected specialization elsewhere.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Graph invariant 2026-06-07-semiconductor-energy-agriculture-chronology.json keeps this removed edge absent and documents the corrected dependency scope.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to mean only nanoscale advanced-materials design after 1974.
The graph split advanced materials into postwar and nanomaterials nodes and retargeted this edge to the latter.
A field taxonomy source showed nanotechnology is a prerequisite parent of all advanced materials science as scoped here.
short_reason: A broad parent field should not depend on one later narrow subfield.
